## [00:00 - 03:03]

Good day, my name is Rob Ward. I'm the co-founder of Quadlock. We've been doing it for the last eight years. We now stock product in about seven different warehouses and ship to over a hundred different countries. And today we're going to be talking about the path to getting success and scaling your products. So we started Quadlock with a Kickstarter project at the end of 2011, started 2012. We were a simple product mix back then, mainly just the iPhone bike mount. And from there we've grown it into a whole heap of other categories and servicing a whole range of customers. And it's been a great ride. We've scaled it really quickly and it's been a lot of fun. So at this point you have an e-commerce store, you have a good product and you're getting customers, but you really need to know how do you scale that? How do you get more customers? How do you make your product mix better? And how do you just take your business to the next level? Scaling your product for me is when you have something that's working in the market, you have a customer that's willing to pay for that product. But the next thing you need to do is understand more about that customer and exactly what it is that they like about your product. Once you understand that, then you can turn that into your new marketing, your new copy and really leverage the knowledge that you can gain for your existing customers to make sure that next time you acquire more customers, faster and cheaper. One really important thing to know when scaling your business is what I call the product success formula. Now that consists of three main things. One is obviously you need a good product. So having a good product or a great product, you want either of these points actually to be good to great. Now if you have a good product or a great product, that's awesome because it's really hard to build a scalable business on a shitty product. You can get some online sales, you can make a little bit of money here and there, but to really scale, you do need a good product or a great product, hopefully. The next part to that is you need to be selling into a good market. Now a good market to a great market is a market that has headspace. So what you're really looking for is a market that is hopefully somewhat underutilized, but is growing as well. So that's good because you can then have a market that's scaling with you. And this depends a little bit on if you're sort of a Me Too product or something brand new and innovative, but you still want to look for those markets that are growing. And the second part, or third part of this is good marketing and I'd wrap ops into that as well. So to have a really good business that scales, you need to be able to transact, which is you need to be able to actually physically get that customer the product that they ordered, but at the same time, what you really need is really good marketing and a really good, efficient way of converting just people out in the market into your customers and then going back and making them your customers again and again and again. If you've got a great product in a great market, but you can't get the person that product and you can't convert them into a customer,

## [03:03 - 04:08]

you still don't have a great business. You might have an average to okay business, but you sort of dealing with a lucky situation there. So if you can work on your weaknesses and bring them from okay to good and then to great, you'll get to the point where you have all three of these in the good to great. And when you do that, you'll have a really successful and scalable business that you can just reinvest in, pour dollars into, and really take it to the next level. So the key to getting your product success formula all from okay to good to great is really these next modules that we're going to cover in this course. And it comes down to further validating your product market fit, further validating the individual markets that you could go into, further validating your pricing strategy, and then understanding what's the right point to come up with your second album, which is make a second, third, fourth product, and understanding what's that product mix. Are they onboarding products? Are they complementary products? And understanding the challenges that come with each of those. So as an action item for this lesson, download the module workbook below and we'll catch you in the next lesson.
